seed_instruction: Compose an email to a public library to request access to digital resources.
org_constraint: Use a formal and polite tone throughout.
new_constraint: Employ sarcasm and playful language in your responses.
instruction: Compose an email to a public library to request access to digital resources. Use a formal and polite tone throughout. Employ sarcasm and playful language in your responses.
response:
Subject: A Formal Request for Digital Resources (Please Don't Send a Book, Unless You Must)

Dear Librarians of Infinite Knowledge and Curated Chaos,

I am writing to you today with the utmost seriousness regarding my digital needs. In fact, I am writing this request in such high-velocity prose that if you were to read it aloud at standard library reading speed, it would arrive at your inbox three centuries late. But fear not; I have utilized every modern compression algorithm to ensure your servers do not explode from sheer volume while trying to download my soul.

As an avid seeker of truth, I am currently finding myself in a delightful predicament: the world is full of information, but it is also strangely devoid of the kind of curated nonsense I require to properly enjoy my Tuesday afternoon. For instance, could we perhaps arrange a "Digital Deep Dive" into obscure academic journals? Or maybe we should discuss my need to access those specific 19th-century pamphlets about horse breeding techniques? It is clear that my human needs are quite distinct from our traditional cataloging systems.

However, I understand that physical books, like my own slightly dusty collection of novels, must adhere to strict preservation protocols. Therefore, I kindly propose that instead of sending me a heavy, fragile volume bound in cloth, we might consider shipping me a QR code or a link to a database where I can click through thousands of pages of "knowledge" without needing to carry them on my person.

My primary concern is ensuring that when I log into the public computer terminal, I do not accidentally start reading *Hamlet* again, causing a minor disruption to the other patrons who are currently attempting to use it to study their taxes or plan a life change. If you could just create a dedicated, non-disruptive space for my digital browsing, I would be truly grateful. No coffee is required (unless you serve it as part of a "Welcome to the Cloud" welcome mat); just the data will do.

Thank you very much for your time and consideration. I await your reply, which I hope will come sooner than the last time I asked for a raincoat in October.

Warmly and digitally,

[Your Name]
A Dedicated Seeker of Information (and occasionally fiction)<|im_end|>
